definition_en: the sudden start of something unpleasant, especially disease or violence
source_sentence: The health ministry declared an outbreak after dozens of new cases appeared within a single week.

definition_en: a tropical disease spread by mosquitoes that causes fever and severe pain in the joints
source_sentence: Travelers to Southeast Asia are often warned about dengue fever during the rainy season.

definition_en: carried and transmitted by mosquitoes
source_sentence: Mosquito-borne illnesses tend to peak in countries with hot, humid climates.

definition_en: a virus passed from one host to another through the bite of an infected mosquito
source_sentence: Dengue is one of the most widespread mosquito-borne viruses in tropical regions.

definition_en: any disease caused by a virus that is transmitted to humans through mosquito bites
meaning_vi: bệnh nhiễm virus do muỗi truyền
source_sentence: Health officials are urging the public to take precautions against mosquito-borne viral infections.

definition_en: an illness caused by a virus entering the body
source_sentence: A simple blood test can confirm whether the patient has a viral infection.

definition_en: an extremely small living thing that causes disease in people, animals and plants
source_sentence: Scientists are racing to develop a vaccine against the virus before the next rainy season.

definition_en: to lose one's life as a result of a particular disease, injury or cause
source_sentence: Hundreds of children still die from preventable diseases every year in low-income regions.

definition_en: a death caused by an accident, war, violence or disease
source_sentence: Authorities reported the first fatality of the epidemic late on Sunday evening.

definition_en: deaths resulting from a particular cause or event
source_sentence: Officials fear that fatalities will continue to rise until the rainy season ends.

definition_en: the end of the life of a person or animal
source_sentence: The death of the patient prompted an urgent review of hospital procedures.

definition_en: a death that occurs as a direct result of dengue fever or its complications
meaning_vi: ca tử vong do sốt xuất huyết
source_sentence: Most dengue-related deaths involve patients who arrive at hospital too late for treatment.

definition_en: the act or process of causing or getting a disease
source_sentence: The infection spread quickly through the densely populated neighborhood.

definition_en: to show, through a medical test, that a person has a particular disease or substance in their body
meaning_vi: có kết quả xét nghiệm dương tính với
source_sentence: Two members of the family tested positive for the virus after returning from their village.

definition_en: actions taken in advance to stop something bad from happening
source_sentence: Spraying drains and clearing standing water are basic preventive measures against mosquitoes.

definition_en: to not have the adequate strategies or resources needed to prevent a problem from occurring
meaning_vi: thiếu các biện pháp phòng ngừa thích đáng
source_sentence: Rural clinics often lack the proper preventive measures to control outbreaks at an early stage.

definition_en: to control something so that it stays within a limited area or does not get worse
source_sentence: Officials are working around the clock to contain the outbreak before it spreads further.

definition_en: medical treatment that helps a patient's body cope with illness rather than curing the disease directly
source_sentence: Doctors provided support care while waiting for the patient's immune system to fight off the virus.

definition_en: the network of hospitals and medical services available in a region or country
source_sentence: The hospital system struggled to cope with the sudden surge of new admissions.

definition_en: having more work or problems than one can deal with
source_sentence: Nurses said the emergency ward had been overwhelmed for weeks.

definition_en: hospitals that cannot cope with the demand placed on them, often because of too many patients or too few resources
meaning_vi: các bệnh viện bị quá tải
source_sentence: Reporters filmed scenes of overwhelmed hospitals turning away non-emergency patients.

definition_en: to make great demands on the strength, resources or abilities of something
meaning_vi: gây áp lực, làm căng thẳng
source_sentence: The unexpected influx of patients began to strain the small clinic's limited budget.

definition_en: to put further pressure on supplies or services that are already limited
meaning_vi: gây áp lực lên các nguồn lực vốn đã khan hiếm
source_sentence: The arrival of thousands of new refugees has strained scarce resources in border towns.

definition_en: supplies, materials or services that are not enough to meet demand
source_sentence: Aid workers must decide carefully how to distribute scarce resources among the hospitals.

definition_en: a situation in which there is not enough of something that people need
source_sentence: A shortage of trained nurses has slowed the response to the crisis.

definition_en: to experience a lack of essential goods, supplies or services
meaning_vi: đối mặt với tình trạng thiếu hụt
source_sentence: Several rural districts are facing shortages of basic medical equipment.

definition_en: liquids given to a patient directly through a vein using a needle and tube
source_sentence: The doctor ordered intravenous fluids to prevent further dehydration.

definition_en: medical treatment delivered through a tube inserted into a vein
source_sentence: Severe cases usually require IV treatment for at least 48 hours.

definition_en: to replace the water that a person or thing has lost
source_sentence: Nurses worked through the night to rehydrate patients suffering from high fever.

definition_en: to restore body-fluid levels in people suffering from dengue fever, usually by means of a drip
meaning_vi: bù nước cho bệnh nhân sốt xuất huyết
source_sentence: Drip stations were set up in corridors to rehydrate patients with dengue who could not be admitted to a ward.

definition_en: to receive medical care, especially over a period of time
source_sentence: More than ten thousand patients are currently undergoing treatment in public hospitals.

term: undergo treatment for dengue in hospitals
definition_en: to receive in-patient medical care for dengue fever inside hospital facilities
meaning_vi: đang được điều trị sốt xuất huyết trong các bệnh viện
source_sentence: Thousands of children are undergoing treatment for dengue in hospitals across the country.
collocation_pattern: undergo treatment for dengue in hospitals

definition_en: a specialist trained in protecting and improving the health of populations
meaning_vi: chuyên gia y tế công cộng
source_sentence: A panel of public health experts will advise the government on the next phase of the response.

definition_en: the most senior officer in charge of a large organization
source_sentence: The director general announced new funding for vaccine research at the press conference.

definition_en: a short meeting at which officials give information to journalists
source_sentence: Reporters gathered for the weekly news briefing on the public health emergency.

definition_en: changes in the world's weather, in particular the fact that it is believed to be getting warmer
source_sentence: Climate change is shifting the seasons in ways that affect agriculture worldwide.

definition_en: the gradual rise in the Earth's average temperature
source_sentence: Coral reefs around the world are dying as a result of the warming planet.

definition_en: the typical ways in which weather behaves over a period of time in a particular place
source_sentence: Farmers have noticed that the region's weather patterns are no longer predictable.

definition_en: the periods of heavy rain that occur each year in southern Asia and nearby countries
source_sentence: Roads in the lowlands often flood during the monsoon seasons.

term: monsoon seasons that are wetter and hotter
ipa: /mɒnˈsuːn ˈsiː.zənz ðət ə ˈwet.ə ənd ˈhɒt.ə/
definition_en: rainy periods that bring more rainfall and higher temperatures than usual, creating ideal conditions for mosquito breeding
meaning_vi: những mùa mưa nóng hơn và ẩm ướt hơn
source_sentence: Climate scientists warn of monsoon seasons that are wetter and hotter than at any time on record.
collocation_pattern: monsoon seasons that are wetter and hotter

term: dengue is common in tropical areas
definition_en: dengue fever occurs frequently in regions with warm and humid climates
meaning_vi: sốt xuất huyết phổ biến ở các khu vực nhiệt đới
source_sentence: Doctors remind travelers that dengue is common in tropical areas during the rainy season.
collocation_pattern: dengue is common in tropical areas

definition_en: the hot, wet region of the world near the equator
source_sentence: Many infectious diseases that were once confined to tropical areas are now appearing elsewhere.

definition_en: conditions that allow animals or insects to produce young
source_sentence: Warm, stagnant pools provide an ideal breeding environment for insects.

definition_en: a place where animals come to produce their young, or a place where something undesirable develops
source_sentence: Abandoned tires can quickly become a breeding ground for disease-carrying insects.

definition_en: water that does not move or flow
source_sentence: Residents were urged to empty buckets and other containers of standing water.

term: store copious amounts of standing water
definition_en: to hold large quantities of water that does not flow away, creating ideal conditions for mosquito breeding
meaning_vi: chứa lượng lớn nước tù đọng
source_sentence: Blocked gutters store copious amounts of standing water on rooftops throughout the wet season.
collocation_pattern: store copious amounts of standing water

definition_en: drainage networks blocked with waste so that water cannot flow freely
meaning_vi: hệ thống thoát nước bị tắc nghẽn
source_sentence: Clogged drainage systems are a constant problem in the older parts of the city.

definition_en: a network of pipes or channels that carry away unwanted water or waste liquid
source_sentence: The city's aging drainage system cannot handle heavy rainfall.

definition_en: having a very large number of people living within a small area
meaning_vi: có mật độ dân số cao
source_sentence: Most of the new infections were reported in densely populated parts of the city.

definition_en: an area of a city that is very poor and where the houses are dirty and in bad condition
source_sentence: Families living in the slum often share a single water tap with their neighbors.

term: living in slums, where sanitation is poor
ipa: /ˈlɪv.ɪŋ ɪn slʌmz weə ˌsæn.ɪˈteɪ.ʃən ɪz pɔː/
definition_en: residing in overcrowded urban areas that lack proper waste-disposal and clean-water facilities
meaning_vi: sống trong các khu ổ chuột, nơi điều kiện vệ sinh kém
source_sentence: Millions of people are living in slums, where sanitation is poor and disease spreads easily.
collocation_pattern: living in slums, where sanitation is poor

definition_en: the systems for taking dirty water and other waste products away from buildings in order to protect people's health
source_sentence: Poor sanitation in low-income neighborhoods has made the epidemic harder to control.

definition_en: the basic systems and services of a country or city that are under serious pressure or difficulty
meaning_vi: cơ sở hạ tầng đang gặp khó khăn
source_sentence: Years of underinvestment have left the country with a beleaguered infrastructure.

term: a city with a beleaguered infrastructure
definition_en: an urban area whose basic systems such as roads, water supply and sewage are struggling to function
meaning_vi: một thành phố có cơ sở hạ tầng đang gặp khó khăn
source_sentence: Dhaka is a city with a beleaguered infrastructure that cannot keep up with its growing population.
collocation_pattern: a city with a beleaguered infrastructure

definition_en: to be the place where a particular group of people, animals or things can be found
meaning_vi: là nơi sinh sống của, là nơi có
source_sentence: Bangladesh is home to more than 170 million people.

definition_en: extremely bad or serious
source_sentence: Patients with severe symptoms were transferred to the intensive care unit.

definition_en: the worst that has ever been recorded
meaning_vi: nghiêm trọng nhất trong lịch sử
source_sentence: Scientists described the heatwave as the most severe in history.

definition_en: moving or growing through an area very quickly
source_sentence: Officials warned residents about a fast-spreading rumor on social media.

definition_en: a time of great danger, difficulty or doubt when problems must be solved or important decisions made
source_sentence: The financial crisis forced the government to cut public spending sharply.

definition_en: becoming worse
meaning_vi: đang trở nên tồi tệ hơn
source_sentence: Doctors are alarmed by the worsening shortage of essential medicines.

definition_en: the fact or condition of being very bad, serious or unpleasant
source_sentence: Reports from the field underestimated the severity of the damage.

definition_en: to make a serious situation even worse
meaning_vi: làm tăng thêm mức độ nghiêm trọng
source_sentence: Power cuts added to the severity of the heatwave for elderly residents.

definition_en: a person or thing likely to cause damage or danger
source_sentence: Antibiotic resistance has become a major threat to global health.

definition_en: an increase in the amount, number or level of something
source_sentence: Officials are investigating the recent rise in respiratory illnesses.

definition_en: to become smaller, fewer or less
source_sentence: Case numbers began to decline after the new restrictions took effect.

definition_en: to affect or be known by more and more people or over a larger area
source_sentence: Misinformation about the disease has spread rapidly online.

definition_en: people who live in the countryside rather than in towns or cities
source_sentence: Rural residents often have to travel long distances to reach the nearest clinic.

definition_en: an area in the countryside, away from large towns or cities
source_sentence: Internet access remains limited in many rural areas.

definition_en: a person who has been forced to leave their country in order to escape war, persecution or natural disaster
source_sentence: Aid agencies are calling for more support for refugees crossing the border.

definition_en: a place where people who have fled their country are temporarily housed
source_sentence: Conditions in the refugee camp have deteriorated since the start of the rainy season.

definition_en: a temporary settlement holding far more people than it was designed for
source_sentence: Disease spreads quickly in crowded camps where families share small shelters.

definition_en: a person who is receiving medical treatment, especially in a hospital
source_sentence: Each patient is screened for symptoms before being admitted.

definition_en: people or organizations with the power to make official decisions
source_sentence: The authorities urged the public to remain calm and follow safety guidelines.

definition_en: people whose job involves making decisions about public health
source_sentence: Health officials issued a warning after detecting a new strain of the virus.

definition_en: a very sharp, intense pain in the head
source_sentence: She woke up with a piercing headache that lasted all morning.

definition_en: the feeling that you are going to vomit
source_sentence: The medication can cause nausea and dizziness in some patients.

definition_en: soreness or aching in the muscles and the places where bones meet
source_sentence: Flu often causes muscle and joint pain that can last for several days.

definition_en: a change in the body that indicates the presence of a disease
source_sentence: Anyone showing symptoms should isolate immediately and contact a doctor.

definition_en: to result in someone dying
source_sentence: Untreated infections of this kind can cause death within a matter of days.

definition_en: a person whose job is to prepare and sell medicines
source_sentence: The pharmacist explained how often the medicine should be taken.

definition_en: the desire or need of customers for goods or services
source_sentence: Demand for face masks rose sharply at the start of the outbreak.

definition_en: to raise the amount of money charged for something
source_sentence: Some sellers increased prices unfairly during the shortage.

definition_en: the amounts of things that are provided or available to be used
source_sentence: Trucks carrying medical supplies arrived at the border on Tuesday.

definition_en: to give materials or goods that are needed
source_sentence: International donors agreed to provide supplies of clean water and food.

definition_en: to send specialists to a particular place to do a particular job
source_sentence: The agency will deploy experts to assess damage in the affected region.

definition_en: sent trained specialists to a specific location to handle or investigate a situation in person
meaning_vi: đã triển khai chuyên gia tại hiện trường
source_sentence: The WHO has deployed experts on the ground to help with case management.

definition_en: to teach doctors the skills they need for their work
source_sentence: The program will train doctors in emergency response techniques.

definition_en: in the place where something is actually happening, not in offices far away
source_sentence: Volunteers on the ground reported that aid was finally reaching remote villages.

definition_en: to examine someone or something in order to find out whether a particular substance or condition is present
source_sentence: Travelers were tested for the virus at the airport.

definition_en: to officially note down or count something for later reference
source_sentence: The agency recorded more than a thousand new cases in a single day.

definition_en: to give people information about something that has happened
source_sentence: Local newspapers reported that schools would remain closed for another week.

definition_en: as stated or reported by someone or something
source_sentence: According to the latest data, unemployment fell slightly last quarter.

definition_en: to be one of the causes of something
source_sentence: Air pollution contributes to thousands of premature deaths every year.

definition_en: several different causes acting together
meaning_vi: sự kết hợp của nhiều yếu tố
source_sentence: A combination of factors led to the collapse of the local fishing industry.

definition_en: to try very hard to deal with a difficult problem over a long period
source_sentence: Researchers have been battling the disease for more than two decades.

definition_en: to have none or not enough of something
source_sentence: The team lacks the experience needed to win at this level.

definition_en: to experience the bad effects of an illness, situation or event
source_sentence: Many older residents suffer from chronic respiratory conditions.

definition_en: to become worse, or to make something become worse
source_sentence: Heavy rain is expected to worsen the flooding overnight.

definition_en: used to introduce bad news or an unwelcome opinion politely
source_sentence: I am afraid that the situation will get worse before it gets better.
